2604.25235.title: VLM Judges Can Rank but Cannot Score: Task-Dependent Uncertainty in Multimodal Evaluation
2604.25235.paper_summary: Vision-language models (VLMs) are increasingly used as automated judges for multimodal systems, yet their scores provide no indication of reliability. We study this problem through conformal prediction, a distribution-free framework that converts a judge's point score into a calibrated prediction interval using only score-token log-probabilities, with no retraining. We present the first systematic analysis of conformal prediction for VLM-as-a-Judge across 3 judges and 14 visual task categories. Our results show that evaluation uncertainty is strongly task-dependent: intervals cover ~40% of the score range for aesthetics and natural images but expand to ~70% for chart and mathematical reasoning, yielding a quantitative reliability map for multimodal evaluation. We further identify a failure mode not captured by standard evaluation metrics, ranking-scoring decoupling, where judges achieve high ranking correlation while producing wide, uninformative intervals, correctly ordering responses but failing to assign reliable absolute scores. Finally, we show that interval width is driven primarily by task difficulty and annotation quality, i.e., the same judge and method yield 4.5x narrower intervals on a clean, multi-annotator captioning benchmark. Code: https://github.com/divake/VLM-Judge-Uncertainty
2604.25235.paper_summary_zh: 視覺語言模型（VLMs）越來越多地被用作多模態系統的自動評審，然而它們的分數並未提供可靠性的指示。我們通過符合預測來研究這個問題，這是一個無分佈的框架，僅使用分數標記的對數概率將評審的點分數轉換為校準的預測區間，而無需重新訓練。我們對VLM作為評審的符合預測進行了首次系統性分析，涵蓋了3位評審和14個視覺任務類別。我們的結果顯示，評估的不確定性在很大程度上依賴於任務：對於美學和自然圖像，區間覆蓋約40%的分數範圍，但對於圖表和數學推理則擴展到約70%，從而為多模態評估提供了定量可靠性地圖。我們進一步識別了一種未被標準評估指標捕捉的失效模式，即排名-分數解耦，評審在產生寬且無信息的區間的同時，達成了高排名相關性，正確排序了反應，但未能分配可靠的絕對分數。最後，我們顯示區間寬度主要受任務難度和註釋質量驅動，即相同的評審和方法在乾淨的多註釋者標題基準上產生了4.5倍更窄的區間。代碼：https://github.com/divake/VLM-Judge-Uncertainty
2604.25235.author: Divake Kumar et.al.
2604.25235.authors: Divake Kumar, Sina Tayebati, Devashri Naik, Ranganath Krishnan, Amit Ranjan Trivedi
